title: Tapping for Diet Guilt
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the guilt that follows eating outside the rules
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the guilt that arrives after eating something outside whatever rules are currently in force. Works through the self-blame pattern, the all-or-nothing thinking, and the emotional cost of treating food as a moral category.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the hours after eating something that triggered guilt or self-criticism.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for diet guilt. The script works through the self-blame that follows eating outside the rules, the all-or-nothing spiral it triggers, and the emotional cost of treating food choices as moral verdicts.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I feel guilty about what I just ate and the voice telling me I've ruined everything is loud, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I keep doing this and then hating myself for it, I am open to treating myself with some kindness anyway.
Even though food has become something I judge myself by, I accept myself right here, in this moment.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The guilt that arrived before the meal was even finished.
Side of Eye: The voice saying I've blown it, I may as well continue.
Under Eye: Food as a test I keep failing.
Under Nose: The cycle: restrict, lapse, shame, repeat.
Chin: How much mental space this has taken over the years.
Collarbone: Treating myself as a problem to be managed through food.
Under Arm: The exhaustion of this relationship with eating.
Top of Head: What food has been asked to carry that isn't really about food.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: One meal is one meal. It has not undone anything.
Side of Eye: I can choose what the next meal looks like. That is always available.
Under Eye: Food does not make me a good or bad person.
Under Nose: The guilt is not useful information. I can put it down.
Chin: My body deserves to be fed without punishment attached.
Collarbone: I am allowed to eat and not spiral.
Under Arm: A gentler relationship with food is possible. I am working toward it.
Top of Head: I release this guilt. It has served no purpose here.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
